texto: In the savage horde the most vagabond, as well as in the most civilized nations of Europe, man is only what he is made to be by external circumstances; he is necessarily elevated by his equals; he contracts from them his habits and his wants; his ideas are no longer his own; he enjoys, from the enviable prerogative of his species, a capacity of developing his understanding bu the power of initiation, and the influence of society.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jean Marc Gaspard Itard sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “In the savage horde the most vagabond, as well as in the most civilized nations of Europe, man is…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, sociology, savage, útil em momentos de reflexao.
